content:
Thank you for your question. Yes, I am confident in my previous answer.

Joe Biden has not (as of my knowledge cutoff in June 2024) been selected individually as Time's Person of the Year. In 2020, "Joe Biden and Kamala Harris" were jointly named Persons of the Year, but Biden has not received the honor alone, which is the context usually implied by such questions.

All other recent presidents—Donald Trump (2016), Barack Obama (2008, 2012), George W. Bush (2000, 2004), and Bill Clinton (1992, 1998)—have each been selected individually. Gerald Ford did not receive the honor during his presidency, but he served before Clinton.

Thus, the correct answer remains:
E
